source_metadata.title_raw: Daniel G. Sodergren - A-19-116 - July 19, 2019 - Pleasanton

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 19, 2019

Daniel G Sodergren
PO Box 520
Pleasanton, CA 94566-0802

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-116

Dear Mr. Sodergren:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding conflict of interest provisions of
Government Code section 1090 ("Section 1090"). Please note that we are only providing advice
under the conflict of interest provisions of Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest
prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code.

Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Alameda County
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that,
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Does Section 1090 prohibit Commissioner Rubino-Brumm from voting on the City of
Pleasanton Human Services Commission's recommendations to the City Council relating to the
award of grants under the Housing and Human Services Grant ("HHSG") Program given that
Sunflower Hill will be submitting applications for such grants and Commissioner Rubino-Brumm is
an uncompensated board member of the nonprofit?

CONCLUSION

No. The noninterest exception set forth in Section 1091.5(a)(8) would allow Commissioner
Rubino-Brumm and the Commission to make recommendations relating to the award of grants
under the HHSG Program so long as her interest is noted in the Commission's official records.

File No. A-19-116
Page No. 2

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You write on behalf of City of Pleasanton Human Services Commissioner Janeen Rubino-
Brumm, an uncompensated member of the Sunflower Hill Board of Directors. Sunflower Hill is a
California nonprofit public benefit corporation exempt from taxation pursuant to Section 501(c)(3)
of the Internal Revenue Code. It has the express mission of developing "a community for
individuals with special needs that provides for life-long residency options and quality
activities/programing – akin to senior living."

The City's HHSG Program distributes funding from the federal Community Development
Block Grant Program, the federal Home Investment Partnership Program, the City's Lower Income
Housing Fee Fund, and the City's General Fund. The Human Services Commission makes
recommendations to the City Council, which awards the grants under the City's HHSG Program.
Sunflower Hill applies for grants from the City in connection with the City's HHSG Program.

ANALYSIS

Section 1090 codifies the common law prohibition against "self-dealing" with respect to
governmental contracts. Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their
official capacities, from making contracts in which they are financially interested. The prohibition
applies regardless of whether the terms of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Thomson
v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.)

Section 1090 is concerned with financial interests, other than remote or minimal interests,
that prevent public officials from exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering
the best interests of their agencies. (Stiwell v. Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) Employees have been
found to have a financial interest in a contract that involves their employment, even where the contract
would not result in a change in income or directly involve the employee because an employee has
an overall interest in the financial success of the firm and continued employment.

The Legislature, however, has created various statutory exceptions to Section 1090's
prohibition where the interest involved is deemed a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091 or
a "noninterest," as defined in Section 1091.5. If a noninterest is present, the public official's
abstention is generally not required, and the contract may be made by the agency.

Noncompensated officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation.

The noninterest exception in Section 1091.5(a)(8) reads as follows:

That of a noncompensated officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt
corporation, which, as one of its primary purposes, supports the
functions of the body or board or to which the body or board has a legal
obligation to give particular consideration, and provided further that this
interest is noted in its official records.

File No. A-19-116
Page No. 3

For purposes of this paragraph, an officer is "noncompensated" even
though he or she receives reimbursement from the nonprofit, tax-exempt
corporation for necessary travel and other actual expenses incurred in
performing the duties of his or her office.

You stated that Sunflower Hill is an Internal Revenue Code 501(c)(3) corporation, and
Commissioner Rubino-Brumm receives no compensation by serving on its board. Additionally,
based upon the description of the services it provides, an express purpose of Sunflower Hill is to
support the function of the City of Pleasure in regard to the care and support of community
members with special needs. Accordingly, if Commissioner Rubino-Brumm notes her interest in the
Commission's official records during Sunflower Hill's funding deliberations, she and the
Commission are not prohibited under Section 1090 from entering into the grant.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Jack Woodside
Counsel, Legal Division

JW:aja

sections.question:
Does Section 1090 prohibit Commissioner Rubino-Brumm from voting on the City of 
Pleasanton Human Services Commission's recommendations to the City Council relating to the 
award of grants under the Housing and Human Services Grant ("HHSG") Program given that 
Sunflower Hill will be submitting applications for such grants and Commissioner Rubino-Brumm is 
an uncompensated board member of the nonprofit?
sections.conclusion:
No. The noninterest exception set forth in Section 1091.5(a)(8) would allow Commissioner 
Rubino-Brumm and the Commission to make recommendations relating to the award of grants 
under the HHSG Program so long as her interest is noted in the Commission's official records.
